branch_excess_unit.base_child_rule: C0(B) is the globally least unpaid child under a fixed total order.
branch_excess_unit.definition: B is an unpaid primitive singleton-heavy top-seam bucket; C is an unpaid child of B under the row r=rho(B) split, excluding the canonical base child C0(B).
branch_excess_unit.identity: outdeg_unpaid(B)-1 = #{C unpaid child of B: C != C0(B)}

canonical_top_seam_boundary_packet.canonical_pair_rule: Choose the globally lexicographically least distance-r boundary pair (S,S') with S in C and S' in B\C.
canonical_top_seam_boundary_packet.note: V=U-c is redundant but retained for verifier clarity.
canonical_top_seam_boundary_packet.source_orientation: S is in the charged child C; S' is in B\C.

does_not_prove: row-sharp Q-prefix atom theorem without branch-payment realization
finite numerical bound for weighted primitive SP/Pade certificates emitted by Rule 2
strict-distance child payment unless it is already a named paid branch
all-depth Route-D row-cell payment without the row-budget hypothesis |R_D|<=t

cross_pair_multiplicity_aware_sp_pade.dedup_rule: Retain the globally least packet per cell after P1/P2; compare every other same-cell packet against that representative.
cross_pair_multiplicity_aware_sp_pade.input: two canonical packets Pi0, Pi with the same cell (r,c) and U != U0
cross_pair_multiplicity_aware_sp_pade.meaning: A same-cell collision with different side polynomials produces a nontrivial weighted low-degree split-pair relation through row r. After cancellation this realizes a multiplicity-aware SP/shift-pair and Padé/Hankel certificate.
cross_pair_multiplicity_aware_sp_pade.paid_status: Rule 2 is realized as a marked multiplicity-aware SP/Padé certificate with exact support cost. The finite numerical payment remains conditional on a printed weighted primitive SP/Padé bound.
cross_pair_multiplicity_aware_sp_pade.predicate: L_+=U0*(U-c), L_-=(U0-c)*U; after H=gcd(L_+,L_-), M_+=L_+/H and M_-=L_-/H are split, coprime, nonidentical, and deg(M_+-M_-) <= E-r-1 where E=deg(M_+)=deg(M_-).
cross_pair_multiplicity_aware_sp_pade.recovery: U = H*M_-/(U0-c), V=U-c, and marked core G recovers the deleted packet.

repeated_side_pair_reuse.dedup_rule: Retain the globally least core for each planted-switch key (r,c,U,beta); noncanonical cores enter planted_switch_core_fiber.
repeated_side_pair_reuse.first_exposed_short_key: (r,c,U) is safe only at the first exposed seam because beta=z is fixed.
repeated_side_pair_reuse.input: two canonical packets Pi_min, Pi with the same planted-switch key (r,c,U,beta)
repeated_side_pair_reuse.meaning: The same local top-seam switch U <-> U-c is reused over the same parent prefix beta with two distinct marked cores.
repeated_side_pair_reuse.paid_status: Rule 1 is proved as exact planted-switch core-fiber descent.  It is paid exactly to the extent that the planted-core fiber ledger includes the printed cost |G_{beta,A}|-1.
repeated_side_pair_reuse.planted_switch_cost: 1 per deleted repeated packet, aggregated as |G_{beta,A}|-1
repeated_side_pair_reuse.predicate: r_min=r, c_min=c, U_min=U, beta_min=beta, and G_min != G

residual_route_d_cell_charge.predicate: packet survives all earlier predicates and is charged to cell (r,c)

strict_distance_child.meaning: C has no top-seam boundary pair and is strict-distance separated from B\C.
strict_distance_child.paid_status: Paid only if the strict-distance Route-D/RIM/window-shadow payment theorem is imported; otherwise remains conditional residual.
strict_distance_child.predicate: d(S,S')=|S\S'| >= r+1 for every S in C and S' in B\C

missing_branch_realization.name: planted_switch_core_fiber_cost
missing_branch_realization.needed_statement: Repeated side-pair reuse with key (r,c,U,beta) is exactly the planted core fiber G_{beta,A}.  The noncanonical repeats are removed from Route-D row-cell mass, but their printed support cost |G_{beta,A}|-1 must be included in a planted/core ledger.
missing_branch_realization.risk: The descent is proved, but it is not zero-cost and cannot be hidden inside image-cell accounting.

missing_branch_realization.name: weighted_primitive_sp_pade_bound
missing_branch_realization.needed_statement: If U != U0 in a common cell (r,c), cancellation produces a marked multiplicity-aware SP/Pade certificate (r,c,U0,G,H,M_+,M_-) with deg(M_+-M_-) <= E-r-1 and exact support cost one.  The weighted SP/Pade dichotomy first-matches cancellation/common-divisor, extension-slope, RIM rank-drop, BC corank-one, and structural quotient/planted classes.  The full-rank primitive residual reduces to fixed-key split-shift sets X_{r,c,U0,H,beta}(z).  The finite ledger still needs a printed bound for their total count, equivalently for N_WSP_full(z).
missing_branch_realization.risk: Exact support-cost realization is not the same as a small printed finite adjacent bound.

missing_branch_realization.name: strict_distance_child
missing_branch_realization.needed_statement: A child separated from the rest of a top-seam bucket by distance at least r+1 is already paid or routed by strict-distance Route-D/RIM/window-shadow rules.
missing_branch_realization.risk: If not already a named branch, this is a new payment obligation.

missing_branch_realization.name: charged_row_budget
missing_branch_realization.needed_statement: All charged top-seam rows r used by the compiler lie in a row set of size at most t, or the theorem is explicitly restricted to the first exposed seam.
missing_branch_realization.risk: Without this, the cell count t*(p-1) does not follow.

open_pr_interactions.position: keeps raw versus masked residual accounting separate; does not convert masked participation-ratio material into Row-sharp Q proof
does not rely on the lift-class cost model refuted by #417
open_pr_interactions.topic: masked participation ratio and lift-class cost model

open_pr_interactions.position: does not introduce Lean theorem-label dependencies or claim lake build/correspondence closure
treats the fixed-key split-shift residual as a shared unresolved object, not as a solved BC/SP payment
does not claim entropy-inverse removal-list completeness; #422-style F_p-span normalization remains adjacent open context
records a local singleton-heavy top-seam compiler and does not supersede a full Route-D residual support certificate
does not use a moment-floor route as a closure; the weighted primitive SP/Pade and row-budget obligations remain explicit

lemma_A_singleton_witness_coverage.statement: At a top-seam bucket of depth m with r=m+1, every singleton child either has a distance-r boundary witness and hence a canonical top-seam packet, or has strict distance at least r+1 to the rest of the bucket.
uses: Q1 distance at common depth m

lemma_B_no_surviving_same_cell_collision.statement: Two surviving canonical top-seam packets with the same cell (r,c) must be identical, after same-side-pair repeats are routed to the planted-switch core-fiber branch and distinct side-pair cross identities are realized as marked multiplicity-aware SP/Pade-Hankel certificates with exact support cost.

requires: charged row set has size at most t
strict-distance child is a named paid branch
Lemma B branch routings are paid and first-matched
lemma_C_live_child_excess_compiler.statement: After choosing one canonical base child at each unpaid top-seam bucket, every other surviving child has a canonical top-seam boundary packet; same-cell collisions are ruled out by Lemma B.

core_fiber.bijection: Pi=(r,c,G,U,U-c;G union A,G union B) <-> G in G_{beta,A}
core_fiber.definition: G_{beta,A}={G subset Omega_AB: |G|=j-r and P_k(G)=beta_k-P_k(A) for 1<=k<=r-1}

dedup.delete: every other core G under the same planted-switch key
dedup.first_exposed_short_key_safe: (r,c,U) only when beta=z is fixed globally.
dedup.why_beta_is_required: For all-depth nodes, the same side key can occur over different parent prefixes beta; those are different planted core fibers.

side_data.disjointness: A cap B is empty because U(x)=U(x)-c=0 would force c=0.

first_match_realization: form weighted Padé normal form F_mu=Y^(r+1)R_D/Q_D
else if quotient-pullback, route to quotient_planted
else if complete-fiber, route to complete_fiber/quotient
else if planted-core data remains, route to planted_switch_core_fiber
else if canonical RIM pivot minor vanishes, route to rank_drop_pivot
else if |D|=r+2, route to BC corank-one chart
else reduce to fixed-key split-shift set X_{r,c,U0,H,beta}(z) for printed counting

abstract_counterexample_family.lesson: SP/Pade shape alone allows many same-key full-rank split-shift members; a KB proof needs a finite split-locator flatness ledger or a new deletion branch.

fixed_key_split_shift_reduction.bound_reduction: N_WSP_full(z) = sum_{r,c,U0,H,beta} |X_{r,c,U0,H,beta}(z)| after canonical first-match partitioning
fixed_key_conditions: U0+H*K is monic squarefree split degree r over Omega
U0-c+H*K is monic squarefree split degree r over Omega
the cross-pair gcd is exactly H
G satisfies parent-prefix compatibility for beta
gcd_decomposition: H=H_A*H_B for exact same-key Rule-2 gcd data
fixed_key_split_shift_reduction.remaining_bound: prove sum_{r,c,U0,H,beta} |X_{r,c,U0,H,beta}(z)| <= B_WSP_full with printed constants
fixed_key_split_shift_reduction.sp_pade_automatic: With M_plus=U0_prime*(V0_prime+H_A*K) and M_minus=V0_prime*(U0_prime+H_B*K), one has M_plus-M_minus=c*K.  Thus the weighted SP/Pade degree condition is exactly the degree bound on K.

step_4_cross_pair_branch_matching.input: same-cell canonical top-seam packets Pi0, Pi with U != U0

multiplicity_aware_sp_match.condition: M_plus and M_minus are monic split polynomials after cancellation; squarefreeness is not required
multiplicity_aware_sp_match.matched_object: multiplicity-aware depth-r weighted sp_shift_pair normal form

pade_hankel_match.non_squarefree_interpretation: non-squarefree reduced sides give weighted Padé/Hankel packets, not branch-shape failures
pade_hankel_match.object: weighted sparse_pade_hankel / finite Hankel-Pade certificate shape

support_exact_cost.cost: 1 support unit per marked multiplicity-aware SP/Padé certificate
support_exact_cost.recovery: U = H*M_minus/(U0-c); V=U-c; marked core G recovers the deleted packet

first_match_classes.consequence: impossible for nonzero reduced mu; route to cancellation/common-divisor if produced upstream
first_match_classes.name: support_collapse_or_common_divisor

first_match_classes.consequence: certificate extends from depth r to depth r+1
first_match_classes.name: extension_slope

first_match_classes.consequence: route to the corresponding named structural branch
first_match_classes.name: structural_quotient_complete_common_planted
first_match_classes.predicate: quotient-pullback, complete-fiber, common-divisor, or planted-core structure is present

first_match_classes.consequence: finite support-level chart count N_WSP_full(z) remains to be printed
first_match_classes.name: full_rank_primitive_weighted_stratum
first_match_classes.predicate: |D|>=r+3, R_D(0)!=0, pivot nonzero, and no structural first-match predicate applies

full_rank_chart.pivot_set: P subset D with |P|=r+1 and nonzero canonical pivot

weighted_sp_pade_dichotomy.rule2_weight_bound: Because L_plus and L_minus are products of two squarefree degree-r locators, residual multiplicities in M_plus and M_minus are at most 2; hence mu(a) in {-2,-1,0,1,2}.

budgeted_all_depth_theorem.branch_realization_scope: strict_distance_child is a genuine named paid first-match branch; repeated_side_pair_reuse is accounted for by the planted-switch core-fiber ledger; cross_pair_multiplicity_aware_sp_pade realizes same-cell distinct-U packets as marked weighted SP/Padé certificates with exact support cost, but still requires a printed finite weighted primitive SP/Padé bound.
budgeted_all_depth_theorem.top_seam_scope: e(B)=rho(B) for every singleton-heavy top-seam bucket B

charged_row_set.definition: R_D = {rho(B): B contributes an unpaid top-seam Route-D branch-excess unit}, where rho(B)=m(B)+1.

deployed_first_exposed_seam.warning: This local specialization only pays first-exposed nodes; all-depth use requires the row-budget hypothesis.
